Right entity (Dexter Gordon, More Than You Know). DNA is verbatim from the Wikipedia page about the 1975 SteepleChase original ('recorded in 1975 ... released on the Danish SteepleChase label' — wiki-supported). BUT ours fields describe the 2025 GleAM reissue (label/year match the MB search release), and the 5 keyTracks (It's You or No One, Hi Fly, Back Stairs, LTD & Intro, More Than You Know) match the GleAM MB tracklist — NONE of these appear on the 1975 album's wiki tracklist (Naima, Good Morning Sun, etc.). So the DNA's 1975/SteepleChase narrative and the actual 2025 product describe arguably different track content under one title. No evidence-grounded contradiction (both fact sets are independently supported), so not factual_error, but a repair writer should reconcile the 1975-original prose with the 2025-reissue fields/tracklist.

Wiki stored_url and MB both about Sun Ra's Sleeping Beauty. All 3 keyTracks match both MB and wiki tracklists. The '2025 swing setting' editorial phrasing is inapt for Sun Ra but is opinion, not a checkable claim.

wrongClaims.ourClaim: As of 2024, he had been nominated for 4 Grammy Awards, including Best Jazz Instrumental Album for Guided Tour in 2014 and Still Dreaming in 2019.
wrongClaims.problem: This Grammy-nomination claim belongs to bassist Scott Colley, not Pat Metheny. It is the stored (wrong-entity) Wikipedia page's content grafted into Metheny's blurb — a disambiguation collision.
wrongClaims.evidenceQuote: Scott Colley (born November 24, 1963) is an American jazz double bassist and composer. As of 2024, he had been nominated for 4 Grammy Awards, including Best Jazz Instrumental Album for Guided Tour in 2014 and Still Dreaming in 2019.

fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: Lullaby of Birdland is a live album by saxophonist Lee Konitz featuring pianist Barry Harris which was recorded at Birdland in 1991 and released on the Candid label.

wrongClaims.ourClaim: Ella and Duke at the Cote D'Azur is a 1967 live album by Ella Fitzgerald, accompanied by the big band of Duke Ellington.
wrongClaims.problem: The entire DNA describes a different album ('Ella and Duke at the Cote D'Azur', an Ella Fitzgerald/Duke Ellington 1967 live record) — not the album titled 'Lullaby of Birdland', which both the embedded-MBID release and the stored wiki page identify as a 1991 Lee Konitz / Barry Harris date on Candid.
wrongClaims.evidenceQuote: Lullaby of Birdland is a live album by saxophonist Lee Konitz featuring pianist Barry Harris which was recorded at Birdland in 1991 and released on the Candid label.

notes: Triple collision. (1) DNA prose is about 'Ella and Duke at the Cote D'Azur', a wholly different album. (2) ours attributes the album to Duke Ellington on Intermedia Records, but the authoritative embedded-MBID release credits Lee Konitz + Barry Harris on Candid, and the stored wiki page agrees. (3) Of 5 keyTracks only 'Lullaby of Birdland' is on the MB tracklist; the other 4 are absent. The wiki link is to the real (Konitz/Harris) album, not the Duke Ellington entity ours claims — wrong relative to our stated entity.

Same artist (Fletcher Henderson) confirmed by the embedded-MBID release, but the authoritative release is the French part-work 'Les Genies du Jazz (Tome 1, No. 10): Fletcher "Smack" Henderson' on Éditions Atlas, secondaryType Compilation. ours label 'De Agostini' contradicts the authoritative MB label Éditions Atlas (both are magazine-series publishers) — factual_error trigger. keyTracks: only 'Sugar foot stomp' and 'The stampede' appear in the MB tracklist; 'Down Hearted Blues', 'Copenhagen', 'TNT' do not. CAVEAT: the title divergence (ours 'Smack' = Henderson's nickname vs the Genies du Jazz volume) suggests the embedded MBID may resolve to a different Henderson compilation than ours describes, which would explain the track/label gaps; a human should confirm whether the MBID was correctly embedded. Opensearch wiki hit was 'Smak' (Serbian band) — discarded; no URL stored.
